message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily because water absorbs colors in the red part of the light spectrum and reflects and scatters the colors at the blue end. When sunlight penetrates the water, red, orange, yellow, and green wavelengths are absorbed more strongly by the water molecules, while blue wavelengths are scattered, much like the scattering of blue light in the sky. This scattering causes the ocean to appear various shades of blue, depending on factors such as the angle of the sun, the presence of algae or sediments
